sections.title: Starting up
Speaking to Yanni Salika
Returning to Yanni without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I supposed to do again?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've forgotten what to do, can you remind me?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Tsk....It's only a little job bwana, I ask you to do me one small favour! Go to the jungle forester and get me a piece of red mahogany. It can't be that difficult can it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is taking longer than I expected!

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: You're telling me bwana, I was supposed to have this wardrobe finished before now! Hurry it up will you, I don't have all day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there an easier way of getting this exotic wood?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Huh! You make it sound like a major undertaking! I should have just done it myself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Everyone is asking me to do them favours now! It seems like I've opened the floodgates!

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Well, can't you just do my favour and forget about theirs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else interesting to do around here?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: You sound a bit bored of Shilo Village...what's wrong?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, I was just wondering if there was anything else to do?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Well, if you're bored, you could do me a small favour and nip to see the jungle forester. I need a piece of red mahogany to resurface an antique wardrobe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, see you in a tick!

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Great, nice of you to do it for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get going then!

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: That'd be helpful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I meet this jungle forester?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I thought you knew the area. You'll find a jungle forester in between the southern edge of Shilo Village and the Kharazi Jungle.

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: They're always trying to find ways into that place.

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: I hear that some old timer from the Legends Guild keeps sending new recruits down there..only Zamorak knows why.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nah thanks, I've got bigger fish to fry.

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Fair enough! Hope it all goes well...but, you know, you looked bored so I just thought I'd suggest it.

sections.title: The various favours are asked
Asking Jungle forester for Red mahogany
Returning to the jungle forester without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Hello friend. You're a long way from Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: I gave you my blunt axe to give to Captain Shanks. I was hoping that he would be able to pop it into Brian's Axes in Port Sarim to get it sharpened. I do hope nothing bad has happened to my axe, I am very partial

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: to it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, yes... okay! No, I'm sure that nothing bad has happened to it. I mean, you gave it to me, I'm really dependable!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to do again?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Take the blunt axe to Captain Shanks and ask him to take it to Brian's Axes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is it that I'm supposed to meet?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: You're supposed to take the axe to Captain Shanks and ask him to take it Brian's Axes in Port Sarim. I'm sure he won't mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you really actually need that axe?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Well, yes, actually. It's my favourite! I've been training with that axe since I started my profession as a jungle forester.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Returning to the jungle forester after losing the blunt axe

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Hello friend. You're a long way from Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost your unsharpened axe, I need a new one.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: I know. Another jungle forester found it and returned it to me. Here, take it again and this time don't lose it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I promise to be more careful.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Hello friend. You're a long way from Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about red mahogany.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Oh, yes, well I can get you a piece but I need my axe sharpening... Do me a small favour would you? Take it to Captain Shanks for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll take your axe to get it sharpened.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Great, here you go! If you could ask the Captain to pop it into Brian's Axes for resharpening when he's next in Port Sarim, that would be really helpful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why should I take it to Captain Shanks?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: He's the Captain of the 'Lady of the Waves', he goes to Port Sarim quite often. Could you ask the Captain to drop the axe in at Brian's Axes the next time he's in Port Sarim? I'm sure he won't mind!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Captain Shanks?

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Wow, you're really not from around here are you? The Captain is to the west of here, follow the northern edge of the Kharazi Jungle, he's usually on board his ship!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Asking Captain Shanks for axe sharpening
Talking to Captain Shanks again

lines.speaker: Captain Shanks
lines.text: Hello there shipmate! I sail to Khazard Port and to Port Sarim. Where are you bound?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I have a favour to ask.

lines.speaker: Captain Shanks
lines.text: I've said no already...now move along...I have a tight ship to run.

lines.speaker: Captain Shanks
lines.text: Hello there shipmate! I sail to Khazard Port and to Port Sarim. Where are you bound?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I have a favour to ask.

lines.speaker: Captain Shanks
lines.text: A favour you say...hmmm. What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have an axe from the jungle forester, I wonder if you could nip into Brian's Axes in Port Sarim and get it sharpened for the jungle forester.

lines.speaker: Captain Shanks
lines.text: Darned cheek! I have a tight ship to run, my friend, and I'll thank you to not take advantage of my good generosity. I have a far superior solution to your problem.

lines.speaker: Captain Shanks
lines.text: Take a ride with me to Port Sarim and take the axe to Brian yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...I was only meant to do a small favour for Yanni Salika...I don't want to go to Port Sarim!

lines.speaker: Captain Shanks
lines.text: Well...the choice is yours! Do like me and never do favours...you'll find yourself in a lot less bother I can assure you!

sections.title: Asking Brian to sharpen the axe
Using the Blunt axe on Bob (optional)

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hmmm, yes, that looks pretty badly damaged. Unfortunately, I don't have the tools to fix it, but Brian over in Port Sarim might be able to.

sections.title: Returning to Brian without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Go talk to Aggie and ask her if she'll be a character witness for my friend before he finds himself in jail with less than savoury people...or worse, he runs off to the wilderness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, right, yes, sorry, I forgot..

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: So it seems!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you sharpen axes?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: You don't have any blunt axes on you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know what! You're absolutely right! I must have dropped it or left it somewhere.

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Well, come back if you need any help with it, we may be able to work something out.

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: You know what, normally I do...but...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... what?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: But I'm a bit preoccupied at the moment. I'm worried that my friend is going to spend the rest of the year behind bars!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds serious!

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Yeah, it is, but it shouldn't be. It was only a harmless pub brawl but he got caught up in it and they just jailed everyone.

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Except he says he's going to run away to the wilderness if he's found guilty and you know what blood-hungry scum inhabit those realms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, can you sharpen this cursed axe or what?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Well, I'd like to. I really would, but I wouldn't be able to concentrate while worrying about my friend so much. I tell you what. If you could do me just one small favour, I promise to help you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, so you want a favour doing as well?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Well, yes, if you wouldn't mind? Aggie the witch is a family friend and she's a pillar of the local community.

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: I know you wouldn't think it would you! Could you ask her if she'll act as a character witness for my friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Oh, please. It would mean such a lot to me! Just ask Aggie if she'll be a character witness for my friend. She only lives in Draynor Village. It'll only take you a few minutes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, ok, I'll do it! I'll go and see Aggie.

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Oh, great, that would be really helpful! Here, give me the axe for safe keeping and I'll get it sharpened as soon as she agrees to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anyone else who can sharpen this axe?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Hmm, well you could try your luck in Lumbridge at Bob's Axes, though I've no clue if he offers this kind of service.

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: He always seemed very focused on selling axes to blood-hungry killers keen to explore the wilderness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Asking Aggie to be a character witness
Returning to Aggie without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Well, I had hoped that you would go and look for 'Jimmy the Chisel'...but it looks like you've had other 'more important' things to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I supposed to look?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: I don't know where he is! But I suggest that you check out that ruined building to the east, you know, the one with all the spiders in it! Doesn't look like anyone's been living in there for years!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I doing this for?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, you mumbled something about 'witnesses', I just can't think straight worrying so much about that poor little Jimmy.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: He could be lying somewhere in a pool of his blood, crying out for help...oooh, it's too much for me to bear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you're a witch aren't you?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: My, you're observant!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I ask you about being a character witness?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Not at the minute I'm afraid...I'm really worried! My new apprentice 'Jimmy the Chisel' has gone missing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Jimmy the Chisel' - what kind of name is that?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: It's sort of like a 'street name'...it's a new fad [player name]...perhaps you've seen it around? Anyway, it's not his name I'm concerned with, it's his whereabouts that worries me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean missing?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Well, I sent him to Lumbridge to get some supplies and he hasn't come back! He left about two days ago and I've seen neither hide nor hair of him all that time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea where he might be?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Not really, I mean, not for definite. But I did hear old Ned talking about some strange activity in that ruined building infested with goblins and spiders in between here and Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: It's probably nothing...but it would aid my mind a lot to know he was safe. I can't leave here until he returns. What if he's had an accident and needs some emergency magical attention?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me guess, you're going to ask me to do you a favour?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Would you my dear, it would only be a small favour...one small favour, that's all I ask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I seem to have heard that one before. Go on then, what do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Could you go on and check out that abandoned building and see if there's anything odd going on. Just check to see if there's any sign of young Jimmy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way...I've had enough of doing favours for people.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Why you big meanie! He could be dead for all you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, Ok, I'll see if I can find Jimmy.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Oh, thanks ever so much...I do appreciate it! Good Luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Asking Johanhus about Jimmy's release
Returning to Johanhus without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Welcome [Brother/Sister]...to our organisation...ours is a fight for humanity in all its shapes and forms...and to rid mankind of the monsters that exist in our world. We have no quarrel with a fellow [brother/sister].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm back.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Welcome back [Brother/Sister] [player name], how are your negotiations going with Brother Fred?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Oh for the love of Saradomin...it's not too difficult! Go to Brother Fred and ask him to supply a month's worth of chickens in return for Brother Jimmy's freedom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't you just release Jimmy the Chisel?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Yes, of course....why didn't you ask earlier? Is that all you wanted? Huh!

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Of course, you do have the chickens don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...not exactly!

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Go get them then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I can do this.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: That's a shame, because Brother Jimmy is getting older every day! His employer isn't going to be too pleased if you don't do something soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Welcome [Brother/Sister]...to our organisation...ours is a fight for humanity in all its shapes and forms...and to rid mankind of the monsters that exist in our world. We have no quarrel with a fellow [brother/sister].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Jimmy the Chisel.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Ah yes, he'll be joining our ranks very soon, I feel. He's still quite young and strong, but he will see the error of his ways before too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh, it sounds like you're forcing him to join you

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Not at all...he just needs some time to think about things...we've given him all the time he needs in the cage back there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have to return him to his employer, she's very worried about him, so if you could just hand him over...I'll be getting out of your way.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't do that...he has value to us...he's related to a farmer named Fred who lives near here. We need to arrange some things with this 'Brother Fred' so that my people down here can eat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I suppose you need me to do you a favour?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Well, actually, now that you mention it...it would be a real help to us if you could organise some chickens in exchange for young Jimmy's release.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, just exactly what is it that you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Go to Brother Fred the farmer and tell him that we have young Brother Jimmy. Tell him that we're not going to let him free until he agrees to send us a month's supply of chickens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way...go do your own dirty work!

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Now, now [player name], I'm a little disappointed in you! Can't you see that this is merely a business transaction?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Come back and see me when you change your mind!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, Jimmy has to be worth more than a few scrawny chickens!

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: That's exactly right! Do this for us and we'll let Brother Jimmy go!

sections.title: Asking Fred for chickens
Returning to Fred before speaking to Seth

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What are you doing on my land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you suggest I do again?

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Have you been to Seth Groats yet? He's over at the other farm on the way to Varrock!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll go and see him then.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What are you doing on my land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about Jimmy.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What's the problem with Jimmy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's being kept prisoner by a bunch of fanatics and I need to organise a delivery of a month's supply of chickens to secure his release.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Oh dear, oh dear, this is terrible. Terrible!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it's pretty bad...but keep calm, I'm going to help.

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: I've no idea what to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: None at all?

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Well, no actually. I'm just a farmer. I've no idea how to deal with fanatics!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, can't you just give me some chickens?

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Normally I'd say yes, but I just don't have enough for a month's supply.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, do you know someone who does have a month's supply?

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Seth does, Seth Groats. He's another farmer over yonder at the other farm. Over the river and on the way to Varrock, he has loads of chickens.

sections.title: Asking Seth Groats for chickens
Talking to him again before talking to Horvik

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: You need to get three bars of steel and take them to Horvik in Varrock, ask him if he can make some chicken cages so that we can transport these chickens to those fanatics and release poor Jimmy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes...I remember now.

sections.title: After accepting a new favour from Horvik

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: How are those chicken cages coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I don't have any yet.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Hello there stranger, what's the meaning of this intrusion? A farmer's hovel is his castle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry for the intrusion, but this is quite urgent. Jimmy the Chisel is being held by fanatics. They won't release him until they get a month's supply of chickens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already talked to Fred but he says he doesn't have a month's supply of chickens. Is there any way that you could help?

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: A month's supply eh! Well, I was going to take them to market and just concentrate on the cows, but I reckon that little Jimmy's neck is worth a few scrawny chickens, don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's exactly what I said...

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Well, Ok then. We need to get this organised. First off, we need to get some proper cages to get them chickens transported. I've never normally transported chickens in such large quantities before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you propose?

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: We'll need to get some proper cages, but I have nothing like that here. You'll have to go and see my friend Horvik in Varrock. He's a pretty well known metal-smith and he's done me a few favours in the past.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: I'm afraid that I'll have to ask a small favour of you as well, my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had a feeling you might!

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Could you get three bars of steel and take them to Horvik, that's what I owe him for a previous job that he did for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way, I'm fed up with this wild goose chase!

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: I understand...

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Poor old Jimmy, locked in that cage...

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Forever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, ok! I guess it's not that much further to Varrock!

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Many thanks! Remember, you need to take him three steel bars.

sections.title: Asking Horvik about chicken cages
Returning to Horvik without medicine

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Hello, do you need any help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I supposed to be doing?

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Please can you get me some medicine? I need herbal tincture and a pot of breathing salts. Also, get me five or so of those pigeon cages that they have over in Ardougne so that I can convert them into chicken

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: cages.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Hello, do you need any help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I need to talk to you about chicken cages! Seth Groats has asked me to come and see you!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Hmm, Seth eh! He's a good friend, but he still owes me three steel bars for a plough I fixed. I'm sorry but I need to have that paid off first before I can do any more work for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have three steel bars at the moment!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: I'm sorry to be so harsh, but I have to ensure the debt is paid before I do any more work for Seth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, well I have three steel bars here, you can have these.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Ok then! Great...tell me a little more about these chicken cages... Cough Cough Cough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear, you don't sound too well?

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: No, I'm not actually, I'm quite ill!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's a shame because I really need your help to make some chicken cages!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: I'm sorry, but the most I can manage today is simple trading...however, if I was to get some medicine say, I could be persuaded to do you a small favour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I see, you need me to do you a favour?

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Well, just one small favour...and then I'll do you a small favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, no not another favour...I just can't do another one!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry about that...but I just can't work when I'm feeling so ill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I guess one good turn deserves another.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Well that's jolly decent of you my friend. I need some medicine, could you get me some herbal tincture and breathing salts, that should solve my ailments!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, hopefully, I'll be right back, but you never know, it might take longer!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: That's great! However, I must warn you, it'll be a lot easier for me to simply adjust some existing pigeon cages. If you could get me five or so of those, that would help a lot.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: But first things first, bring me the medicine!

sections.title: Asking the apothecary for medicine
Speaking to the apothecary after refusing to go to Barbarian Village

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about the breathing salts?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I smashed the pot which held them, remember?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes...where am I going to get a new pot?

sections.title: Returning to the apothecary without the airtight pot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that herbal tincture you gave me!

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: No problem, I'll just rustle up another one for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Go and see Tassie in the Barbarian Village about making an airtight pot for me to store my breathing salts in, then you can take some for Horvik.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, yes right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Horvik is ill, I need breathing salts and herbal tincture.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: No problem!

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: There you do! There's your herbal tincture!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that was surprisingly easy! Fantastic, thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: No problem, pleased to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...but...what about the breathing salts...

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Oh, yes, breathing salts...erm, just let me look around. I'm sure I have some around here somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok...

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Yep, here they are....

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Oh blimey...I've dropped the pot and smashed it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's ok, just hand over the breathing salts.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Oh no, you don't understand. They're ruined. They're supposed to be kept in an airtight container and this was my last one.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I can't order any more in until I have a new airtight container. And they were a top selling item as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just knew this wasn't going to be easy! So you can't get any more breathing salts?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: No, I can..I can order plenty, but they'll lose their effectiveness. I need an airtight pot to keep them in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would you get one of those from?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I've no idea really, this pot was a family heirloom. I guess you'd have to get a new one made?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where could I do that?

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: You could try Tassie Slipcast over at the Barbarian Village. She's a potter there and may be able to make one. But mind, it has to be air tight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, no way am I going to the Barbarian Village.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: I understand...they're all barbarians over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I can go to the Barbarian Village.

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Wonderful, I'd really appreciate it!

sections.title: Tassie's request
Returning to Tassie without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there...I'm back...

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: So I see. What's the problem? Did you sort out Hammerspike?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not exactly, can you tell me what I'm supposed to do again?

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Go talk to Hammerspike in the Dwarven mines and see if you can get him to leave me alone...I won't be able to make you the airtight pot else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there...I need an airtight pot just as soon as you can make one!

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: I beg your pardon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a matter of some urgency that I have an airtight container as soon as possible!

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Who on earth do you think you are coming in here demanding an airtight pot...Can't you see that I have my own worries to think about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I didn't think...what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Well, if you must know, I'm working all the hours that Saradomin sends, I have to in order to make enough money to pay that loan shark Hammerspike!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Loan shark? Hammerspike? What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: I took over this abandoned pottery place with a loan from Hammerspike. He's demanding payment and I can barely just make the interest payments.

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: I'm working as hard as I can but I'll never pay the loan off at this rate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I need an airtight pot...to store breathing salts in.

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Well, that may be, but I have a rush order for a top paying customer that's going to keep Hammerspike off my back for a bit.

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: If you really want that pot, you're going to have to deal with Hammerspike in the dwarven mines!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll deal with Hammerspike!

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Really, you will?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll go and have a chat with him, perhaps we can sort something out.

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Well, that would be really great...I just hope he doesn't grind you into flour to make his bread, he's one of the most ruthless gangsters in the area.

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Beware, his guards are incredibly loyal, they'll slit their own throats to protect him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not a pretty picture you're painting, but don't worry, I'll be careful. Now, where do I find this Hammerspike?

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: He's in the dwarven mines somewhere, not sure exactly where though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, no more blooming favours for anyone!

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Oh, well that's a real shame, I was hoping you could have sorted out that Hammerspike for me once and for all!

sections.title: Dealing with Hammerspike
Returning to Hammerspike without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: And what's wrong now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it that I was supposed to do for you?

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Go and talk to Sanfew and get him to accept me as a druid initiate!

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: And who da heck are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [player name] and I want to talk to you about a friend of mine, Tassie Slipcast. She says you're putting the screws on her for some loan she had from you. Is this true?

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: And what if it is? Tassie got into an arrangement that is mutually beneficial, as far as I understood it, she was happy with the deal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've come to change the deal!

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Oh, so you wanna give me something for leaving Tassie alone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...er...not exactly...why can't you just leave her alone? I mean, make a nice gesture, I'm asking out as a favour...please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't you just leave Tassie alone?

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Where would I be if I just left all of my 'customers' alone? Some people just need an extra 'encouragement' to make the best out of life...I'm just helping them along.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, come on...do something nice for a Tassie. Think about how hard she's working!

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Well...now that you come to mention it, I guess I have been a little hard on the girl.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, come on give her a break.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: And I guess I am quite stressed about things, what with so much to think about recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can understand that...you have a lot on your mind. Go on, make a change...it'll do you good! There must be something else that you wanted to do at least once in your life.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Hmm, yes, yes you're right...I did want to do something, something I noticed recently, but you'll laugh if I tell you...nah, it's silly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you always been a gangster? Wasn't there anything else that you wanted to do instead? Come on, tell me what you want to do...I'm listening.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Well, I was just thinking the other day that I'm feeling sort of unfulfilled in my life.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: I'd like to get rid of all this anger and frustration that my current lifestyle brings and do something relaxing...like those druids, I mean, all day sitting around, looking at herbs and wearing robes and stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, that sounds good.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, I really want to be a druid...so...go and see the druid leader, I think Sanfew is his name, and persuade him to take me as an initiate. Do me this one small favour and I'll leave Tassie alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think they take dwarven gangsters as initiates.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Whatever...I guess Tassie's gonna be working hard for a bit longer yet then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, another favour...I think I can manage that.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Good...I mean great! Come back when Sanfew has agreed to take me as an initiate!

lines.speaker: Dwarf gang member
lines.text: I can't believe [he/she/they] fell for that, boss!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Asking Sanfew to take Hammerspike as an initiate
Returning to Sanfew without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you need me to do again?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Make a herbal tea for the gnome and persuade him to take me to the ogre area, down to the south!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, right, thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make the tea again?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Ok, you need to heat some water up in a bowl, then pour it into an empty teacup. Add 1 harralander, 2 guam and 1 marrentill...it'll warm that poor fellow up a treat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, right, thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you taking any new initates?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Hmmm, are you considering yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does someone become an initate?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Hmm, it's quite a straight-forward process. A person, perhaps like yourself, comes along to me and asks.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: We test your knowledge of Herblore and see if you can fit in with the rest of us. If you pass, then we let you become an initiate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems pretty straight forward!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you accept dwarves?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Dwarves? Well now, that's a mighty interesting question... Just because we haven't before, doesn't mean to say that we shouldn't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A dwarf I know wants to become an initiate.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Oh yes? Well, that's interesting. Let me think for a moment. Hmmm... yes, actually, I don't mind taking him on, so long as you can do me just one small favour. Well, two actually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not you as well?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Stop interrupting me. I haven't told you about these favours!

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: There's a poor little gnome up on White Wolf mountain. I wondered if you could take him a warming herbal brew. I have the recipe here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that seems simple enough.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: In addition...

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: I've long wished to take a trip down to see those ogres to the south of Yanille, and bring the word of Guthix to them. I was hoping you might organise this trip with the gnome pilot up there on White Wolf mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you mad?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Well, perhaps a bit... but it's never stopped you doing things for me before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been asked to do a lot of favours recently.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Well, that's the life of an adventurer. Is it a deal, or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, it's a deal. Tell me the recipe for this herbal tea.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Ok, you need to heat some water up in a bowl, then pour it into an empty teacup. Add 1 harralander, 2 guam and 1 marrentill...it'll warm that poor fellow up a treat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mmm, sounds delicious! What's it called?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: We call it 'Guthix's rest'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I drink it?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: If you want!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! And thanks.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: But you'll have to make another one for the gnome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I can drink it though.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Yes, but you'll have to make another one for the gnome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I think I understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, it's not a deal!

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Well, that's a shame. I was looking forward to visiting the ogres... Perhaps someday I will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Bleemadge without Guthix's rest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sanfew asked me to come and talk to you.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Brrr, blimey it's cold up here! Wish I had something nice to drink to chase away these chills!

sections.title: Giving Bleemadge the tea and talking about Sanfew's favour
Before accepting Bleemadge's favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How was that tea?

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: It was delicious thanks!

sections.title: Returning to Bleemadge without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Go to Arhein in Catherby and get some T.R.A.S.H! That's a short name for Triple Redundant Aero Super Hawser and then bring it back to me! I mean, it's not difficult is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a special tea here for you from Sanfew!

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Ohhh! Many thanks! Brrr, blimey it's cold up here!

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Blimey, it was certainly jolly decent of that old stick Sanfew to get you to make me a cup of herbal tea. Wow! It really does chase away the chills!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, he's quite considerate that way...you know, thinking of people. In fact, he was thinking that you may be able to help him with another little 'helping' situation that involves ogres.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Ogres!

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Hmmm, well I guess they're just like overgrown gnomes when you think about it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: More like 'super-over-grown-gnomes' if you ask me! Or 'overgrown humans'!

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Quite! Anyway, go on! What was Sanfew after?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sanfew wants to bring the word of Guthix to those ogres, though I've no idea if ogres have the mental ability to grasp druidic concepts.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Yes, quite, but who amongst us does? I know I'm often confused by that beardy bunch of yakking druids.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, he wants to know if he can organise a glider ride down there.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Hmm, interesting! I know that the elders want to extend the network of glider routes, and I did hear that the Feldip Hills were being considered. I don't know why it hasn't opened up yet though.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: If I'm going to transport that yakking druid down to the ogres I'll want to make it in record time so I'll need some lighter kit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: There's a chap called Arhein in Catherby and I've heard that his special interest in ships has helped him to produce lightweight but strengthened ropes. I'm in need of some T.R.A.S.H.! And I need you to get it for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: TRASH? What the demonikin are you talking about? Why do I have to get it?

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: I'm talking about the next generation of lightweight ropes. Triple Redundant Aero Super Hawser...T.R.A.S.H.! I'll transport your yakking druid if you can get me some T.R.A.S.H!

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: And you have to get it because I have to stay here with this glider. Is it a deal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Go to Arhein and ask him for some T.R.A.S.H....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is T.R.A.S.H?

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: T.R.A.S.H. is a short way of saying, 'Triple Redundant Aero Super Hawser', it's a technical term that we gnomes like to use when specifying lightweight rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get this from?

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Go and talk to a chap called Arhein in Catherby, he's developed it so he'll know exactly what you're talking about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! So I get to talk T.R.A.S.H with shopkeepers!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll go and get you some T.R.A.S.H.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Wonderful my friend, wonderful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, I can't do it right now.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Well, that's a bit of a shame...I was looking forward to upgrading my glider!

sections.title: Talking T.R.A.S.H. to Arhein

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello! Would you like to trade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk T.R.A.S.H. to you.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Oh yes, well it won't be the first time...but just so I'm sure can you explain what you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course. There's a Gnome on White Wolf mountain who needs some T.R.A.S.H...for his glider...do you have some?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Well, I've done a lot of research into Triple Redundant Aero Super Hawsers and it seems to have paid off, but answer me this one question. Why are you asking me this rather than the Gnome?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm sort of doing him a favour.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hmm, really, is that so? Well, I do have some Triple Redundant Aero Super Hawser and I'll let you have some if you do me just one small favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How totally unpredictable!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: No, this is a really quick thing and shouldn't take you longer than a few minutes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I've heard that before! Go on...tell me anyway.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I'm funding a rather expensive trade run to Brimhaven soon and I want to be sure that the weather isn't going to change and cause my ships any trouble.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: The last thing I need is a load of goods going straight to the bottom of the ocean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I can understand that.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I just need you to pop along to Seers Village and ask those philosophers of foresight for a weather report. I believe a new seer there called Phantuwti can help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way...I've had enough of this wild goose chase.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Ok, I'll do it!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: That's great, many thanks.

sections.title: Asking Farsight for a forecast
Talking to Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you need me to do again?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: I need you to start checking nearby caves looking for our missing Petra. The caves are probably home to some creature or creatures. Check around the vicincity.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: The impressions are coming from a south westerly direction. Don't go further east than you are already, no further south than Ardougne and no further west than the combat training camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, can you give me a weather forecast?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Hmm, somehow I knew you were going to ask me that...strange...life is so predictable when you have the power of foresight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! So I can assume you've been really pro-active and compiled an up-to-date one for me?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Alas my presumptuous friend, the power of foresight and the impulse to act is quite similar to the interplay between intelligence and wisdom.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: One might have intelligence enough to realise that cakes are bad for us, but we may not have the wisdom to stop cramming that delicious-looking fatty cream cake into our salivating mouth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, in your case, you have the power of foresight, but you're too lazy to act on it?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Not exactly lazy...but unable. My abilities and concern have been focused on the fate of a local girl whose association with a certain wizard has landed her between a rock and hard place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is this girl that you're concerned about?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: The girl's name is Petra, she's a very ...energetic youngster, always wanted to travel and have adventures. When she turned seventeen she set off to earn herself fame and fortune.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: It looks like she's ended up in a bit of a dead end position...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is this girl?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: It's quite difficult to pinpoint exactly where she is, but apparently she's in some sort of cavern structure.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: We sense that there are other beings around her, smelly creatures, and some sort of presence is watching over her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any ideas where that might be? I mean, it's pretty vague isn't it?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, we believe it may be some sort of nearby cave, it can't be very far away as the impressions we get are quite strong. We believe the cave is a home for some sort of creature or creatures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think needs to be done?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, it would be nice to locate her...if possible. Then we may be able to work out what to do when we can see what sort of state she's in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry but I can't help.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, that's a bit of a shame, I have to say...we all miss our Petra!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do to help?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Do you think you could do me a small favour? Check around the vicinity. The impressions are coming from a south westerly direction.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Don't go further east than you are already, no further south than Ardougne and no further west than the combat training camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, let me get this right. You want me to start poking my nose into a lot of caves where some creatures might be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Risking my neck for some tomboy who thinks she's got what it takes to be a real adventurer?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, I wouldn't have put it quite that way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, Ok, I'll do it.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Great! I just knew you'd agree to help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, I can't do it right now. Or maybe ever!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: I knew you wouldn't help...oh well, perhaps someday you'll surprise me.

sections.title: Searching for Petra in the Goblin Cave
Walking near the Sculpture
Looking at the Sculpture
Searching the Sculpture
Asking Wizard Cromperty for a spell
Returning to Wizard Cromperty without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Hello there. My name is Cromperty. I am a wizard, and an inventor.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: You must be [player name]. My good friend Sedridor has told me about you. As both wizard and inventor, he has aided me in my great invention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Go to Port Khazard and get me some iron oxide from Tindel Marchant.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Hello there. My name is Cromperty. I am a wizard, and an inventor.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: You must be [player name]. My good friend Sedridor has told me about you. As both wizard and inventor, he has aided me in my great invention!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about a girl stuck in some rock!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Eh!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: What? I have no clue what you're talking about! Now, please excuse me! I need to get back to my experiments!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! Not so fast! I've seen a young girl encased in rock, a note in her belt led me to you.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Oh dear, it's such a bother getting them back out of the rock walls and I do loathe the trip to Port Khazard for the components.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the demonikin are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Well, it's not the first time someone's been teleported into a rock face, just the slightest miscalculation and bang...they're in the wall!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: It's fairly straightforward to cast a spell and fish them out again - but it requires a spell component from a chap in Port Khazard.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: I don't suppose you could do me a small favour could you?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Just one small favour? Pop along to Port Khazard and get some iron oxide, it's a component of the spell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I going to Port Khazard for you!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Oh dear, what a shame...I just don't have time to deal with this now...she's safe where she is for now, and I'm sure I'll get to it sooner or later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh! Ok, one more 'small favour' isn't going to kill me...I hope!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Waaahaaay! Great! I'd never suspect you'd be so helpful!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: You need to talk to a chap called Tindel Marchant in Port Khazard, he's sort of like an antiques dealer.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Well, off you go then! A journey of a thousand miles starts with the first step and all that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Are you saying I have to travel a thousand miles?

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Of course not...you silly...just...you know...the sooner you start the sooner you can get back...that's all I was saying.

sections.title: Asking Tindel Marchant for iron oxide
Returning to Tindel Marchant without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Hello there! Welcome to my special antiques boutique.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: You need to take that stodgy mattress I gave you to Rantz and ask him to fill it with feathers! Then, bring it back to me and I'll give you some iron oxide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay then!

sections.title: Returning to Tindel Marchant after losing the mattress

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Hello there! Welcome to my special antiques boutique.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, I've lost the mattress... do you have another one I can get refilled?

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Yes, but I had to pay for this myself so you'd better pay me for losing my last one...it cost 100 gold!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here's 100 coins for another mattress...

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Okay, thanks a lot, here's the mattress!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not paying you 100 coins for that stinky mattress.

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Well, don't expect your iron oxide any time soon then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, then I won't.

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: GLADLY!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Grrrr!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Hello there! Welcome to my special antiques boutique.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wizard Cromperty sent me to get some iron oxide. Do you have some?

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: As a matter of fact, I do! However, it's the last in stock and I'm not likely to get more for a while so it's highly prized amongst the wizards at the moment.

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: It's not easy to get so much rust together, even if I do come across it more often than most people with cleaning these rusty swords up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rust? What do you mean? How much does it cost?

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Yes, iron oxide is more commonly referred to as rust. As for a price, hmmm...well...let me think now...I could give it to you for the price of...

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: One small favour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Groan!!!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: I need a nice mattress to sleep on! I haven't had a decent night's sleep in an ice-age...could you take this stodgy mattress down to Rantz in the ogre area and ask him to fill it with prime chompy bird feathers please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, let me get this straight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to traipse ALL the way down past the ogre city with some flea infested mattress, to some hermit ogre, who can't shoot a chompy bird to save his life, so that you'll give me a pot of rust?

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Well, er, I wouldn't have said it quite that way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll do it!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Great, many thanks indeed! Here's the mattress!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I just don't want to do it.

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Well, that's a shame, but come back and see me if you change your mind.

sections.title: Asking Rantz the ogre for a mattress
Returning to Rantz without completing the favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Yous creature, go see little guy and help wiv da man flapper 'come-down-place'...den Rantz helps yous creature wiv da fluffsies sack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to talk to you about a mattress!

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: You's creature shuvs flapper sack in rantz face, making rantz very upset

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you fill this mattress with feathers?

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Der's no flappers to get cos da little guy scares dem away wiv his banging. He's making da man flapper 'come-down-place' and it's takin' ages and ages.

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Da chompies don't come no more since he's banging all da time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on around here?

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Da little guy, he's making da man flapper 'come-down-place'...so deyz more of yous creatures coming to see Rantz...but he's takin ages to do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do about that?

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: You's can help da little guy, he's over der near da ogre city...he's stuck wiv sumfing, making da man flapper 'come-down-place'. Me's not doin' no chompy hunting wiv dat noise going on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Dat would be da good find...den me's help wiv da flufsies sack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Helping Gnormadium with the gnome glider
Before repairing the lights

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you wanted me to do again?

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Go and take a look at those landing lights and see if you can get them to work, would you? They've been as dull as an ogre conversation since we started working here.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Come back and tell me if you actually manage to get them fixed.

sections.title: Removing the uncut gems
Obtaining a chisel

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Hello! Don't get in the way around here, we've got a lot of work to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a chisel to fix these crystals.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Okay, I can sell you one for 10 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have 10 coins.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: So I can't sell you one then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll buy a chisel for 10 coins.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Okay, many thanks for your custom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I won't buy the chisel.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Fair enough, come back if you change your mind.

sections.title: Obtaining more uncut gems

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a new [opal/jade/red topaz] to fix the lights.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Broken something, have you? I'll want 500 coins for a new one. Be careful - my supplies are limited, and if I run out, you'll have to find your own materials elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have 500 coins!

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: I can't give you one then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here's 500 coins, I'll buy one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I paying that much.

sections.title: Fixing the lights
After fixing the lights

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fixed all the lights!

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Hmm. That seems a tad unlikely to me, but let's flick the switch here and see, shall we?!

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: I don't believe it - you fixed it! Well done, well done... We can pack up now and leave these ogres in peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know one ogre who'll be very pleased about that!

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Hello! Don't get in the way around here, we've got a lot of work to do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, what are you working on?

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Well, it's quite exciting... we're extending the glider network to include the Feldip Hills so that people can come and look at these curious ogres.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: They are impressive creatures aren't they - quite mystified they are by the technology we gnomes have invented.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you working on?

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Well, people will be able to visit this area, once construction has finished, by using the most advanced network of glider routes in the whole of Gielinor... in fact, the only network of glider routes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything that I can do?

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Not really, I'm afraid, it's probably all far too technical for someone like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rantz said I should help you finish this project.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Rantz? *gulp* Well, that would be nice actually! We are having some problems with getting the landing lights to work... do you think you could take a look at them?

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: I expect that it's far too complex for you, but hey, you may be able to do something!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll take a look at them.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Ok then, just pop over and let me know if you're not sure what you're doing... I'm sure I'll just have to fix it myself anyway. If you need any materials, just pop over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I've got other things to attend to.

lines.speaker: Gnormadium Avlafrim
lines.text: Yes, I expected it might be a bit too complex for you. We Gnomes lead the way with technical things like this - world leaders, you might say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Heading back to complete the favours
Returning to Rantz after helping with the gnome glider
Talking to Rantz after losing the comfy mattress

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that filled mattress you gave me!

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Yous creature needs to bring me da saggy fluffsies sack to full wiv da chompy flufsies!

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: It cost you dis many bright pretties to do dis again, yous den be more careful wiv my work next time round.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have that many bright pretties!

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Den yous no get da fluffsies sack...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll give you 200 bright pretties.

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: De're yous goes creatures...dere's yous comfy fluffsies sack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I giving you 200 bright pretties.

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Den yous no get da fluffsies sack...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I've helped that Gnome, he shouldn't bother you anymore.

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: Yous creature dun a gud fing...Rantz give yous creature da fluffsies sack now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have the stodgy mattress anymore...I'll have to get another one.

lines.speaker: Rantz
lines.text: De're yous goes creature...da fluffsies sack is full now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I think Tindel will get a good night's rest on this.

sections.title: Returning to Tindel with the feathered mattress
Talking to Tindel after losing the iron oxide

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Hello there! Welcome to my special antiques boutique.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the iron oxide, can I have some more?

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Okay, but I'm going to have to charge you I'm afraid. 200 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I haven't got 200 coins!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Sorry, but I have to make a living.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll buy it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I paying 200 coins for a bottle of rust!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Suit yourself... I have plenty of other customers.

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Hello there! Welcome to my special antiques boutique.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the mattress!

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Great! Let's have a look then.

lines.speaker: Tindel Marchant
lines.text: Great! Here's the iron oxide I promised you!

sections.title: Returning to Wizard Cromperty with the iron oxide
Talking to Wizard Cromperty after losing the animate rock scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need another animate rock spell.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: I suspected that you might...you can have one, but it will cost you 100 gold pieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have 100 coins!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: So you can't have the spell then can you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way...I'm cleaning up your mess, you should be grateful!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Well, I did give you the last one for free...you're not getting another one for free, even if it is my mess that you're trying to clear up! You shouldn't be so careless to leave things lying around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, here you go!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Thanks...try not to lose it this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Grrrrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that iron oxide you asked for!

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Great! Just what I needed! Now I need to give you a spell scroll to help bring Petra back again.

lines.speaker: Wizard Cromperty
lines.text: Just cast this in the room, it should have her out of that rock in a jiffy! Good luck with your exploits...now, where was I with that wranklin severance device...

sections.title: Freeing Petra
Using the animate rock scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um nahi listic durooo-rah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whoooah! That was a bit of a misfire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And this doesn't look good!

sections.title: Talking to Petra

lines.speaker: Petra Fiyed
lines.text: Oh thank goodness you released me...how long have I been stuck in that gloomy wall?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, not so long...I don't think...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean...that is... I'm not so sure...at all...in fact.

lines.speaker: Petra Fiyed
lines.text: Well, at least I'm ok...thanks to you! Last time I get involved with that Cromperty lunatic...in fact I have some words to say to that man!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well actually, I think that some of the people at the Seers Village are worried about you. That's how I came to find you here, Phantuwti sent me.

lines.speaker: Petra Fiyed
lines.text: Oh, right, yes, well I guess I'd better go back there and let them all know that I'm safe then..Ok, thanks...I'll be off now and once again thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem...I'll probably see you back at the Seers Village.

sections.title: Going back to Farsight after saving Petra
Examining the weather vane with a full inventory
After repairing the weather vane

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fixed the weather vane!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Oh, thanks so much...you can't believe how helpful it is to get accurate predictions on the weather...we Seers are almost blind without it.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: There you go...a weather report as asked for...I hope it helps you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've released Petra, she should have returned.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Hi there, yes she has actually! Well done, you're a true hero!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, can I have the weather report now please?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: I wish I could but it's just been so difficult to get a clear image of the future at the moment. You know...we're all so relieved that Petra has returned and all that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Listen you...just give me the blooming weather report!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Er...no. I can't, I told you, I can't get a clear picture. Nope, sorry, the conditions are just not quite right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why can't you get a clear picture?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, it's just like that Ok, I just can't get a clear picture...and that's it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've done my part of the deal, now hand over that weather report.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: I would really like to, please believe me, I would, but I just can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll run you through if you don't give me that weather report.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, that's not very nice...I mean, I'm a nice guy and I thought you were nice too! Why would you want to run me through? I mean, it's only a weather report.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: It doesn't make sense to get angry about something so trivial.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're making me angry! I did you a favour, now pay up!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: I'm not making you angry, you're doing all that on your own! I just don't have a weather report to give you!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: I can't help that, you should be more understanding and less forceful, then I might be persuaded to give you the weather report.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, it's complicated, the special Seers tools that help us predict the weather are not aligned properly...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which special Seers tools do you mean?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, we use a special combination of items which are used to gauge current weather movements and we're able to predict future weather patterns from these. Of course, it's strongly linked to the Seers' foresight ability.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean, 'special combination of items'?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, let me think now...There's a copper eye, a rotating thing, and some pointy direction thingies. It's all highly scientific - and of course mystical - in a very magical 'foresight' sort of way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're talking about a weather vane aren't you?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Er...maybe...I may be talking about that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't predict the weather because your weather vane is broken...that's it isn't it? Holy Saradomin breath....you can't even predict the weather, you need a weather vane!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Erm, well....err....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, show me where this weather vane is...I want to look at it.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: What for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be able to fix it and then you can give me a weather report...come on, let's go!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Oh, very well..but you'll need to be a specialist to fix this weather vane, it was made by some of the most experienced....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just show me it!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Ok, ok, it's up on the roof...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're talking in riddles, just give me a straight answer!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Since when has any Seer ever given you a straight answer...the power of knowing the future and the visions we see have to be wrapped in allegory so that the truth can slowly be accepted by the listener.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't allegory though, you're just avoiding my questions!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: How can you be sure? Are you so sure of what you just asked me? And am I really avoiding your questions by asking you lots of other questions? Answer me that huh!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Anyway, we need a special combination of items before we can truly have foreknowledge of the weather.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How hard can it be to predict the weather?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Quite hard actually. I mean, you're still waiting for me to do it aren't you and I've told you time and again that I can't...right now...but that normally it's actually quite an easy thing for me to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, what's it going to take to get a weather report?

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Hmm, well, you can come back in several months and see if we can do one for you....does that help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, it doesn't help at all, I need a weather report and I need it now...so hand it over!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're just a fake...you can't predict the weather!

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Why...how dare you...I've never been so insulted in my life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should get out more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it going to take for you to give me that weather report.

lines.speaker: Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight
lines.text: Well, nothing in fact, as I can't give you a weather report. I'm sorry, but that's just the way it is.

sections.title: Giving Arhein the weather report

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the weather report for you.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Fantastic...let's see it!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Wonderful...well the weather report is slightly unspecific, but I feel much better anyway for getting it. Hopefully now the Seers will take some responsibility if my ships go down to the bottom of the ocean.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I'll get one of my associates to take some T.R.A.S.H to your Gnome Glider Pilot friend on White Wolf mountain. Once again, many thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Completing Bleemadge's favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there, did you get your T.R.A.S.H? Arhein said that he'd send one of this associates up with it.

lines.speaker: Captain Bleemadge
lines.text: Oh, yes, thanks...I've already installed it. I should go like the wind now! Tell Sanfew, that yakking old druid, I'll be happy to take him to the ogre area now...and yourself of course if you should like to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok then...thanks I will!

sections.title: Completing Sanfew's favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there, the gnome Pilot has agreed to take you to see the ogres!

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: What's that? He's agreed to take me...well that's wonderful! Many thanks.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Please tell that dwarf that he's welcome to come here and train to be an initiate.

sections.title: Returning to Hammerspike
After defeating Hammerspike's men

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Well...sure if you're not full of surprises then...I guess I'll have to either hire more men to try and kill you or forget Tassie's loan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, you promise me that you won't bother Tassie anymore and I'll leave you alone.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Ok then, fair enough....I'll leave her alone...but I'm going to deal with you personally one of these days!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be ready for you whenever you want to take a shot Hammerspike...I'm not afraid of you! You're just a bully who can't fight his own battles.

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Oh, it's you! Back are you then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep and with good news...your life is about to change for the better. I've persuaded Sanfew to take you in as an initiate!

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: Ha, ha ha ha! How funny is that! I've gone and changed my mind now! I've decided that I make a far better gangster than I ever would a druid!

lines.speaker: Hammerspike Stoutbeard
lines.text: My men are going to deal with you!

sections.title: Completing Tassie's favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there, Hammerspike won't be bothering you anymore!

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Really! Fantastic! Oh, I'm so grateful! Thanks so much! How ever can I repay you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you could make me an airtight pot!

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: I'll do better than that! I'll show you how to make an airtight pot! First of all, you'll need some clay...here's some I have spare!

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Now, while pots are quite easy to make, lids are a bit different. They have to be quite precise to fit the pot exactly, which is why you have to be quite experienced to make them.

lines.speaker: Tassie Slipcast
lines.text: Ok then, just use it on the wheel over there!

sections.title: Returning to Apothecary with the sealed pot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there! I have an airtight pot for you!

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Wonderful, let me see!

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: Huzzah! You have an airtight pot! Wonderful! Ok, let me get some breathing salts into this thing for you!

lines.speaker: Apothecary
lines.text: That's great! Many thanks friend...here's your breathing salts!

sections.title: Completing Horvik's favour
Returning to Horvik only with herbal tincture

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the herbal tincture.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Well that's no good, I need the breathing salts as well!

sections.title: Returning to Horvik only with breathing salts

lines.speaker: Player
