lines.text: I have the breathing salts.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Well that's no good, I need the tincture as well!

sections.title: Returning to Horvik with tincture and salts

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Great! Let's have a look at them then!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Wonderful! That's just great! I just need the pigeon cages now.

sections.title: Returning to Horvik without cages

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: I need you to get me five pigeon cages, apparently you can get them from Ardougne. When I have them, I can convert them into chicken cages for you.

sections.title: Returning to Horvik with the cages

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the five pigeon cages you asked for!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Great stuff...let's have them here then! Ah, I can turn my hand to a bit of woodworking on these, great, I do it as a hobby.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: There you go then! There's your chicken cages!

sections.title: Losing chicken cages

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost a chicken cage!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Well, Ok, I'm going to need a pigeon cage to convert it, plus some money for my time, say a 100 gold?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have a pigeon cage.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Sorry, but you'll have to get one so that I can convert it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have 100 gold...

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Sorry, but I have to charge for my time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll pay, here's the pigeon cage and 100 gold.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: There you go then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Many thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I paying for that!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Suit yourself then!

sections.title: Handing Seth the chicken cages
Returning with less than five chicken cages

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: How are those chicken cages coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some chicken cages!

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Well, I need at least five! Come back when you've got that many!

sections.title: Returning with all five chicken cages

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: How are those chicken cages coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here they are! I've got all five!

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Well that's great! Well done! You can go and talk to those fanatics now and tell them that they'll get their chickens and they can let little Jimmy the Chisel go! Once again, thanks for your help.

sections.title: Talking to him after getting the chickens but before rescuing Jimmy the Chisel

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Thanks for getting us those chicken cages...you can go and talk to those fanatics now and get Jimmy released.

sections.title: Talking to Fred the Farmer after delivering the chicken cages

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: What are you doing on my land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry about Jimmy. His release is imminent!

lines.speaker: Fred the Farmer
lines.text: Oh, that's wonderful...many thanks for your help...just wonderful.

sections.title: Completing Johanhus' favour

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Welcome [Brother/Sister]...to our organisation...ours is a fight for humanity in all its shapes and forms...and to rid mankind of the monsters that exist in our world. We have no quarrel with a fellow [brother/sister].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're in luck, I've managed to swing that chicken deal for you.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Hmmm. Congratulations on your achievement, your help will ensure the success of our venture and we can surely take our efforts forwards. Tell me, won't you consider joining our calling?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the offer, but I'm sort of in the middle of something currently.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Right...well please consider it an open offer...Ok then...thanks and goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hold on! Aren't you forgetting something?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: What? Sorry? Please calm down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said that you would release Jimmy! Are you going to keep to your word or am I going to have to get angry?

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Oh my dear friend...please...there's no need to get so upset...of course we're going to release Jimmy. Oh dear, you do take things a bit seriously don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good...I have things to do and I've had enough of people messing me about.

lines.speaker: Johanhus Ulsbrecht
lines.text: Ok, I understand...please don't worry. I'll release Brother Jimmy now! Tell his employer that he'll be there shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well...but if you're lying, I'll be back and I won't be happy!

sections.title: Completing Aggie's favour

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news! Jimmy has been released by those fanatical HAM people! He said he was on his way back here!

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Yes! He's just returned! I'll happily be a character witness for Brian now! Please let him know that I'll help his friend however I can.

sections.title: Completing Brian's favour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've returned with good news. Aggie has agreed to be your character witness!

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Well, I wish I could be more happy, but I've just had some bad news.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Well, it seems that my friend was even more desperate than I had originally realised. He's gone to hide away from the authorities in the wilderness...what a lunatic!

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: I've heard that he's wandered into the lava maze in the most dangerous part of the wilderness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...what's going to happen now?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Well, I was wondering if you could do me just one small favour? Please could you go and fetch him back again for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have got to be kidding!

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Well....yes...I was....actually!

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Here you go, here's your axe! And very many thanks for your help and a good laugh! You should have seen your face! Oh what a picture!

sections.title: Talking to Brian after losing the axe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that sharpened axe you gave me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I get another one please?

lines.speaker: Brian
lines.text: Sure, here, you go.

sections.title: Returning to Jungle forester with the sharpened axe
Losing the red mahogany logs

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Hello friend. You're a long way from Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that red mahogany you gave me!

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Well, I can get you another piece but you're going to have to pay for my time... 200 coins I'll want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't have 200 gold coins!

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Sorry friend, I know you did me a small favour, but I have to earn a crust too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here's 200 coins for some red mahogany.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Many thanks my friend!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't pay you 200 coins for a piece of wood!

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Well, suit yourself, I can always find other customers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news, I have your sharpened axe!

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Ah, great!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, here it is... at long last!

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Yeah, you've been a while... I mean, I could have just gone and done it myself! It probably wouldn't have taken so long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why you ungrateful...

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Hang on a minute... there's no need to get so angry. Here's that red mahogany you wanted.

lines.speaker: Jungle forester
lines.text: Okay, thanks for getting my axe sharpened for me!

sections.title: Finishing up
Returning to Yanni Salika with the red mahogany

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's the red mahogany you asked for.

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Well, that's absolutely wonderful my friend! Many thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, that's it?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Well...what were you expecting? I mean, it took you so long I could have gone and got it myself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What! I've traipsed halfway around Gielinor...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've negociated with fanatics, dwarven gangsters and arrogant gnomes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fixed ancient weather vanes and gnomish landing lights and fought supernatural rock monsters...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've filled stodgy mattresses with feathers, made pot lids to help cure an ailing metal-smith...and that's all the thanks I get? 'Thanks!'

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Well, you didn't have to go to so much trouble...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Raarrrhhhh!

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Look, I can see that you're quite upset about this...so here, have this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: It's a key ring, you can store some of your most valuable keys on it...helps to keep things organised!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A key ring? That's it?

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Well, I only asked you to do me one small favour...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Groan! Yeah, so did everyone else!

lines.speaker: Yanni Salika
lines.text: Oh....ok, I guess I could offer you these lamps...I'm not sure what they're worth, but you can have them.
